_comment.1: SOUNDCARD DELAY TEST (y axis). The LEFT channel (vector[0]) drives the
_comment.2: FSM y/tip; RIGHT is not connected (verified 2026-07-22 two-tone L20/R30:
_comment.3: 20 Hz -> y at 8x, 30 Hz at noise floor). Because the soundcard is AC-
_comment.4: coupled, a STEP decays back to baseline, so latency_test's settled-step
_comment.5: calibration aborts (step << 8 sigma). The correct delay measurement is the
_comment.6: BODE PHASE SLOPE: bode_plot injects a sine into command element 0 (y),
_comment.7: aylp_alsa scale [1,0] sends it to L, and the beam's y CoM response is read
_comment.8: back. fit_tau_ms + loop_frames in the log/dat IS the command->error delay
_comment.9: of the soundcard->FSM->camera->CoM path (project's trusted delay method).
_comment.10: freq_start 12 Hz keeps the fit ABOVE the AC high-pass corner so the phase
_comment.11: is a clean pure-delay slope (below the corner the high-pass adds phase
_comment.12: lead and biases tau). aylp_alsa in LOW-LATENCY threaded mode (0.083 ms
_comment.13: queue) so the actuator adds ~0 delay, matching the intended loop. Piplate
_comment.14: PARKED holding the 2 V bias so the FSM sits in range. Run under sudo for
_comment.15: rt_prio/mlockall. Read: fit tau_meas_ms and loop_frames from the log.

pipeline._comment: Inject a sine into command element 0 (y) and read the y CoM back.

params.config: SOUNDCARD y/tip delay: aylp_alsa L (hw:0,0 S16_LE 48kHz, threaded 0.083ms) -> analog sum -> FSM y-fine, coarse bias 2/2, 32x32 exp200 bw100. Piplate parked (scale 0) holding DC bias. R channel not connected.

pipeline._note: SOUNDCARD under test, LOW-LATENCY threaded. scale [1,0]: drive ONLY L (=vector element 0, the y command bode injects); R muted (not connected). latency_frames 4 = 0.083 ms queue so the actuator adds ~0 delay.
Piplate PARKED: scale 0 drives nothing, offset 2 holds the DC bias so the FSM sits in its linear range while the AC-coupled soundcard sweeps.
